main_products: AMR Systems, Easy Pack, Palletizer, Ingetrans, Retal (Scrap Management), Service & Maintenance, Custom Engineering Projects
main_customer_segments: Corrugated packaging plants, Box converters/printers, Large packaging groups (Saica, DS Smith, Smurfit WestRock, Hinojosa, Klingele), USA packaging integrators
main_competitors: CORE: Ducker, Harmax, Funce, Serra, Cermex, TOSA Group, Cartec, Boix Europe, Columbia Okura | AUTOMATION: ProMach, Coesia, ABB, KUKA, ATS | OEM INDIRECT: Bobst, Krones, Syntegon, IMA | LOW COST: Asian integrators
current_challenges: 85% revenue from custom projects with low predictability; limited and unstructured service business (only 2,000h/year in 50km radius); reactive sales model; small commercial team; dependence on large Ingetrans deals; slow standard product market adoption; no structured pipeline management
strategic_goals: 1. Grow from €2.5M to €3.5M annually by 2028. 2. Shift to 80% standardized products (€2.8M), 14% custom projects (€0.5M), 6% services (€0.2M). 3. Build recurring service revenue engine targeting €1.1M+ at scale. 4. Establish Top-10 account program in Spain. 5. Expand proactively into USA and Europe markets. 6. Transform from reactive to pipeline-driven commercial model.
additional_notes: Owner & GM: Diego Garcia. Co-owner & Admin: María Jesús Garcia. Team: 2 Back Office, 1 Mechanical Engineering Leader, 1 Electrical Engineering Leader, 1 Maintenance Leader (Paco), 2 Technicians. 70% internal / 30% outsourced cost structure. Transformation plan 2026–2030 designed by Iñaki Senar (v1.0, Jan 2026). AFCO ecosystem = 65+ target accounts in Spain. Strategic positioning: 'Faster than OEMs · More reliable than low-cost'.
business_description: Ingecart is an agile industrial engineering integrator specializing in end-of-line automation, intelligent material handling, and intralogistics for the corrugated packaging industry. Based in Subirats, Barcelona (Catalunya), the company provides standardized product solutions — AMR systems, palletizers, Retal scrap management, Easy Pack, and Ingetrans heavy transport — as well as custom-engineered projects with fast, measurable ROI. With 9 employees and strong OEM-agnostic engineering DNA, Ingecart is positioned as 'faster than OEMs, more reliable than low-cost' for corrugated plants across Europe and the USA.
objectives: 1. Reach €3.5M annual revenue by 2028 (from current €2.5M). 2. Shift revenue mix: 80% standard products / 14% custom projects / 6% services. 3. Scale service capacity from 2,000 h/year to 24,500 h/year potential (14 technicians target). 4. Activate Top-10 account development program with structured KAM. 5. Deploy USA and European agent network. 6. Launch 3 productized offer packages: Quick Throughput Pack, Smart Changeover Cell, Greenfield Assist. 7. Establish weekly sales discipline (45-min cadence) and monthly/quarterly business reviews.
strategy_context: Three-pillar growth model: (1) Standard Products — core engine targeting €2.8M with 4-6 AMR, 5-7 Easy Pack, 6-10 Retal, 1-2 Palletizer per year; (2) Custom Projects — selective at €0.5M/year, strategy or innovation-driven; (3) Services — recurring revenue from maintenance contracts, spare parts, remote support, growing from €0.2M to €1.1M+ at scale. 18-month roadmap Phase 1 (0-3m): brand + foundations; Phase 2 (4-10m): Top-10 workshops, ABM campaigns, ROI-first pilots; Phase 3 (10-18m): scaling programs. Commercial governance: Weekly Sales (45min pipeline), Monthly Business Review (P&L + risks), Quarterly Business Review (strategy refresh).
market_context: Corrugated packaging market driven by e-commerce growth, plant consolidation post-M&A (IP×DS Smith, Smurfit×WestRock mergers), PPWR/EUDR regulation, OEE pressure, and automation investment. Spain: AFCO ecosystem ~65+ member companies representing ~93% of national production. Priority targets: Tier 1 groups (Saica, DS Smith, Smurfit WestRock, Hinojosa, Klingele) for corporate entry; converting/box plants (La Plana, Santorromán, Ondupack, Onducart) for core recurring revenue. Indirect channel via OEM subcontracting (Valmet, Voith). Ingecart competes at 3 layers simultaneously: agile integrators (core), large OEMs (high CAPEX), and Asian low-cost (price pressure). Strategic sweet spot: plants with a problem that need fast ROI, not corporate procurement.
